notes:
RetinaTag 1.5.74 — P1 freeze + data-integrity fixes

Fixed:
- Face recognition no longer freezes the UI (recognize_all_faces wrapped in spawn_blocking)
- Watch folders import doesn't block other DB commands anymore (lock released across slow I/O)
- Batch rename is transactional with on-disk rollback — no more 'photo disappeared' after a half-applied rename
- Vault decrypt: disk read errors are now reported as disk errors, not as 'vault corrupt' (so users don't run recovery on a recoverable vault)
- Search pipeline race: fast typing in a large library no longer lands on results for an older query
- Toast / toastAction now HTML-escape their messages (future-proofs against error strings with < or &)

From v1.5.73:
- Vault P0 leaks fixed (batch / toggle / auto-NSFW now encrypt files before flipping private=1)
- Single-instance plugin
- XSS hardening (filenames, tags, descriptions)
- selectPhoto await-race guard, unsaved description auto-save

From v1.5.72:
- Large-library launch freeze (spawn_blocking + sequential init)

Windows: x86_64 NSIS installer.
macOS: Apple Silicon (M1/M2/M3+).
